combination.camera_slot_description:
Camera is at true floor level, looking upward with a low-angle perspective.
Wide or standard architectural lens (18-24 mm equivalent).
Oblique tilt looking up from below the artwork centerline.
Three-quarter oblique rotation matching the wall perspective.
The artwork must remain protected at its precomposed scale and perspective, with the environment generated around it.
Keep the artwork sharp.
Camera object integrity: The artwork is precomposed at the supplied physical dimensions before environment generation. The mask protects the artwork face and physical footprint.
Camera negatives: no centered product symmetry, no artwork substitution, no warped artwork, no bent artwork, no stretched artwork, no compressed artwork, no floating artwork.
combination.final_prompt_preview:
Generate one premium photographic mockup using this exact experimental camera slot only:
Camera Slot ID: combo_17
Camera Slot Name: Combo 17 - Inpainting Vertical

EXPERIMENTAL INPAINTING STRATEGY:
Use an inpainting-first reading: IMAGE 1 must be treated as the protected root artwork, precomposed at real physical scale before the room is generated. The environment may be rebuilt around it, but the artwork face, physical footprint, aspect ratio, orientation, and relative size must remain locked.

INPUT ROLES:
IMAGE 1 is the only source of truth for the artwork. Preserve exact identity, colors, mark placement, sparse areas, surface rhythm, proportions, and format.
IMAGE 2 is the world mother and the authority for environmental DNA: wall material, floor material, architectural mass, light temperature, and mood. Reproduce IMAGE 2's material and light identity faithfully. Do not copy its camera angle, layout, furniture placement, wall choice, window placement, crop, or perspective.

ARTWORK PHYSICAL DATA:
Artwork physical size: 80 cm wide x 120 cm high.
Artwork depth: supplied physical canvas/object depth metadata only.
Artwork orientation: portrait.

CAMERA:
Create a low-angle perspective looking upward. The artwork is installed on the wall at its true physical size. The wall, ceiling, furniture, and shadows must look realistic and integrated around the protected artwork.

SCALE LOCK:
Keep that real scale. Do not enlarge it to dominate the room, do not make it mural-sized, billboard-sized, wall-filling, room-divider sized, or architectural-panel sized.

MASK PROTECTION:
Keep the artwork surface unchanged. Do not repaint, reinterpret, alter, crop, mirror, rotate, recolor, simplify, extend, replace, blur, stretch, compress, bend, curve, melt, or recompose the protected artwork.

INTEGRATION:
Generate the surrounding wall, floor, ceiling, light, contact shadows, and architecture around the protected artwork. Add subtle physical shadow and contact depth at the artwork edges. The artwork must feel genuinely installed in the room, not pasted or floating.

NEGATIVE PROMPT:
No eye-level view, no centered product wall view, no symmetrical monument view, no mural scaling, no billboard scaling, no oversized canvas, no room-dominating artwork, no wall-filling panel, no changed artwork scale, no changed artwork format, no rotated artwork, no stretched artwork, no compressed artwork, no warped artwork, no bent artwork, no melted artwork, no floating artwork, no artwork substitution, no invented painting, no copied painting from IMAGE 2, no room-inspired marks on artwork, no classical painting substitution, no portrait replacement, no landscape replacement, no visible measurement labels, no visible text, no logos, no watermarks, no pets, no crowds, no children.
